item.title: In Artforum’s March 2026 issue, Joanna Mytkowska remembers art historian, critic...
item.description:
🖼️ Slideshow (7 medier)

@artforum

In Artforum’s March 2026 issue, Joanna Mytkowska remembers art historian, critic, gallerist, and curator Anka Ptaszkowska (1935–2025), who passed away last November. “We bid farewell to Anka and her singular approach to the world, which revolved around demanding the impossible and refusing to compromise,” Mytkowska writes. “Her implacable defense of freedom and autonomy in communist Poland and internationally manifested in her affinity for events that were accidentally beautiful, rather than planned.”

Read Mytkowska’s tribute through the link in bio.

Images:
Anka Ptaszkowska at Vitrine pour l’Art Actuel, Paris, August 1979. Photo: Eustachy Kossakowski.
Foksal Gallery “We See You” window with photos of Tadeusz Kantor, Zbigniew Gostomski, Maria Stangret, Wiesław Borowski (left sash), and Anka Ptaszkowska, Henryk Stażewski, Edward Krasiński (right sash), Warsaw, 1969. Photo: Eustachy Kossakowski.
Theoretical Girls performing at Vitrine pour l’Art Actuel, with Anka Ptaszkowska standing in the front row, Paris, 1978. Photo: Eustachy Kossakowski.
Anka Ptaszkowska with her dog, Peggy, during the seminar “Principe d’égalité” (The Principle of Equality), Ecole des Beaux-Arts, Caen, France, ca. 1993–95.
View of “Anka Ptaszkowska: Case by Case,” 2023, Museum of Modern Art in Warsaw. Photo: Daniel Chrobak.
#JoannaMytkowska #AnkaPtaszkowska #Situationism #ArtforumMarch2026

Se slideshow på Instagram
item.content:encoded:
🖼️ Slideshow (7 medier)

@artforum

In Artforum’s March 2026 issue, Joanna Mytkowska remembers art historian, critic, gallerist, and curator Anka Ptaszkowska (1935–2025), who passed away last November. “We bid farewell to Anka and her singular approach to the world, which revolved around demanding the impossible and refusing to compromise,” Mytkowska writes. “Her implacable defense of freedom and autonomy in communist Poland and internationally manifested in her affinity for events that were accidentally beautiful, rather than planned.”

Read Mytkowska’s tribute through the link in bio.

Images:
Anka Ptaszkowska at Vitrine pour l’Art Actuel, Paris, August 1979. Photo: Eustachy Kossakowski.
Foksal Gallery “We See You” window with photos of Tadeusz Kantor, Zbigniew Gostomski, Maria Stangret, Wiesław Borowski (left sash), and Anka Ptaszkowska, Henryk Stażewski, Edward Krasiński (right sash), Warsaw, 1969. Photo: Eustachy Kossakowski.
Theoretical Girls performing at Vitrine pour l’Art Actuel, with Anka Ptaszkowska standing in the front row, Paris, 1978. Photo: Eustachy Kossakowski.
Anka Ptaszkowska with her dog, Peggy, during the seminar “Principe d’égalité” (The Principle of Equality), Ecole des Beaux-Arts, Caen, France, ca. 1993–95.
View of “Anka Ptaszkowska: Case by Case,” 2023, Museum of Modern Art in Warsaw. Photo: Daniel Chrobak.
#JoannaMytkowska #AnkaPtaszkowska #Situationism #ArtforumMarch2026

Se slideshow på Instagram
item.author: artforum

item.title: SUMMA MALLORCA 2026 brings together prominent international galleries, artists, ...
item.description:
🖼️ Slideshow (6 medier)

@artforum

SUMMA MALLORCA 2026 brings together prominent international galleries, artists, and collectors on the island for an unmissable encounter with contemporary art. It is a space for meeting, discovery, and dialogue in the heart of the Mediterranean, where new artistic proposals and creative visions take center stage.
____

SUMMA MALLORCA 2026 reúne en la isla a destacadas galerías, artistas y coleccionistas internacionales en una cita imprescindible con el arte contemporáneo. Un espacio de encuentro, descubrimiento y diálogo en el corazón del Mediterráneo, donde nuevas propuestas artísticas y visiones creativas toman protagonismo.

Se slideshow på Instagram
item.content:encoded:
🖼️ Slideshow (6 medier)

@artforum

SUMMA MALLORCA 2026 brings together prominent international galleries, artists, and collectors on the island for an unmissable encounter with contemporary art. It is a space for meeting, discovery, and dialogue in the heart of the Mediterranean, where new artistic proposals and creative visions take center stage.
____

SUMMA MALLORCA 2026 reúne en la isla a destacadas galerías, artistas y coleccionistas internacionales en una cita imprescindible con el arte contemporáneo. Un espacio de encuentro, descubrimiento y diálogo en el corazón del Mediterráneo, donde nuevas propuestas artísticas y visiones creativas toman protagonismo.

item.title: On view through August 9 at M+, “Lee Bul: 1998 to Now” is a comprehensive survey...
item.description:
🖼️ Slideshow (6 medier)

@artforum

On view through August 9 at M+, “Lee Bul: 1998 to Now” is a comprehensive survey of the South Korean artist’s career. Featuring major works from Lee Bul’s studio and collections across Asia and beyond, the exhibition traces the pivotal developments and visual language of one of Asia’s more prominent contemporary artists. Plan your visit via @mplusmuseum.

Images:
“Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now,” 2026. Installation view, M+, Hong Kong, 2026. Photo: Lok Cheng.
“Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now,” 2026. Installation view, M+, Hong Kong, 2026. Photo: Dan Leung.
“Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now,” 2026. Installation view, M+, Hong Kong, 2026. Photo: Lok Cheng.
“Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now,” 2026. Installation view, M+, Hong Kong, 2026. Photo: Wilson Lam.
“Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now,” 2026. Installation view, M+, Hong Kong, 2026. Photo: Wilson Lam.
“Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now,” 2026. Installation view, M+, Hong Kong, 2026. Photo: Lok Cheng.

Se slideshow på Instagram

🖼️ Slideshow (6 medier)

@artforum

On view through August 9 at M+, “Lee Bul: 1998 to Now” is a comprehensive survey of the South Korean artist’s career. Featuring major works from Lee Bul’s studio and collections across Asia and beyond, the exhibition traces the pivotal developments and visual language of one of Asia’s more prominent contemporary artists. Plan your visit via @mplusmuseum.

Images:
“Lee Bul: From 1998 to Now,” 2026. Installation view, M+, Hong Kong, 2026. Photo: Lok Cheng.

item.author: artforum

item.title: “Across his multimedia practice, Kamrooz Aram’s project is one of disrupting the...
item.description:
@artforum

“Across his multimedia practice, Kamrooz Aram’s project is one of disrupting the habits of classification that render aesthetic judgment perfunctory,” writes Julian Stern. An installation by the Iran-born, Brooklyn-based artist is currently on view in the 2026 Whitney Biennial. In Artforum’s April 2026 issue, Stern considers Aram’s “Variations on Glazed Bricks,” a 2021 series that investigates the kind of discourse “that Edward Said critiques for furnishing the West with authority over the East, an instrument of cultural and geopolitical domination.”

Link in bio.

Images:
Kamrooz Aram, Variations on Glazed Bricks, 2021, oil, colored pencil, and book pages on linen, five parts, each 20 × 16".
Kamrooz Aram, Descendants (Luster on Blue Glaze), 2025, oil, pencil, and book pages on linen, two parts, overall 24 × 40″.
Kamrooz Aram, Arabesque Composition (Archipelago), 2025, oil, oil crayon, and pencil on linen, hinged with artist’s frame, five parts, overall 7′ 5/8″ × 10′ 11 1/2 × 1 1/4″.
Bricks with palmette motif, Achaemenid period, Susa, Iran, ca. 6th–4th century BCE, ceramic, glaze, 7 1/2 × 5 1/2 × 3 3/8″.
@j.sterno @kamroozegar @whitneymuseum #WhitneyBiennial #ArtforumApril2026
item.content:encoded:
@artforum

“Across his multimedia practice, Kamrooz Aram’s project is one of disrupting the habits of classification that render aesthetic judgment perfunctory,” writes Julian Stern. An installation by the Iran-born, Brooklyn-based artist is currently on view in the 2026 Whitney Biennial. In Artforum’s April 2026 issue, Stern considers Aram’s “Variations on Glazed Bricks,” a 2021 series that investigates the kind of discourse “that Edward Said critiques for furnishing the West with authority over the East, an instrument of cultural and geopolitical domination.”

Link in bio.

Images:
Kamrooz Aram, Variations on Glazed Bricks, 2021, oil, colored pencil, and book pages on linen, five parts, each 20 × 16".
Kamrooz Aram, Descendants (Luster on Blue Glaze), 2025, oil, pencil, and book pages on linen, two parts, overall 24 × 40″.
Kamrooz Aram, Arabesque Composition (Archipelago), 2025, oil, oil crayon, and pencil on linen, hinged with artist’s frame, five parts, overall 7′ 5/8″ × 10′ 11 1/2 × 1 1/4″.
Bricks with palmette motif, Achaemenid period, Susa, Iran, ca. 6th–4th century BCE, ceramic, glaze, 7 1/2 × 5 1/2 × 3 3/8″.
@j.sterno @kamroozegar @whitneymuseum #WhitneyBiennial #ArtforumApril2026
item.author: artforum
